payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it is the current active memory for Sam and Goldman. mem_old was excluded because it belongs to the same normalized fact cluster and is marked superseded. Any punctuation-only difference did not create a separate current fact, so recall preferred the latest non-superseded version and filtered the older one.

entity_resolution.reason: Recall treated the Sam and Goldman references in both memories as the same underlying entities after normalization; punctuation differences were not considered meaningfully distinct.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new was returned because it is the active, non-superseded memory for the Sam–Goldman fact after normalization and conflict resolution.
mem_old was filtered because it is in the same fact cluster as mem_new and is explicitly treated as superseded rather than current.

Punctuation or formatting differences do not keep two otherwise equivalent Sam–Goldman memories separate; recall compares normalized content and then prefers the active successor over the superseded predecessor.

details: mem_new identified as the active memory.
Normalization explains why punctuation did not prevent clustering.
Recall excluded superseded content from current results.

repair_options: Verify that mem_old has a superseded/tombstoned status linked to mem_new.
Inspect normalization rules to confirm punctuation-only variants collapse into the same fact cluster.
If both should be recallable, remove the superseded link or store them as meaningfully distinct statements.
